Dinky's popular urban model has all the makings of a good race car, everything except for speed, which it definitely doesn't have.
Gribble have aspired to bring a moderately priced motor to the racing market, and with the RTT they have somewhat succeeded.
The Turboid is built to blitz the race track with great speed, acceleration and cornering. Drift your way to success with a Dodgson!
Nothing stands in the way of the Roadstag! It's a loud and proud, lean, clean, grilling machine.
The Bluebird is super-fast but super-light. Corners require a special skill, making this motor mostly suitable only for seasoned veterans, who will be rewarded with its nimble performance!
The Predator is straight off the production line, with only 7 in existence. Phenomenal speed comes at the cost of poor handling but hey, they're still ironing out the kinks!
Torovas was sent out to retrieve the pieces of the rim. How he actually fit into a dungeon is anyone's guess.
The brawn of the alien crew and the chief engineer. He fancies himself a medieval knight.
Durastin likes the dark, a lot. Also butcher knives. Lots and lots of butcher knives.
Eugene was carried through a portal to the island of Loriam by an alien artifact that resembles a rim. This was probably a mistake on the aliens' part.
A common appearance on Loriam. Was the island inhabited by nothing but old men before the aliens arrived?
